title: Trump administration restricts Fable/Mythos to America only, Anthropic pulls the models in response
description:
A government just tried to make a way of thinking a national weapon. Here's why that fails.

Anthropic has pulled its most capable models - Mythos and Fable - offline for everyone, after a national-security order moving to restrict the most powerful AI to U.S.-only access. So I want to talk it through, in the framing of Shared Sapience and The Century Report, because this sits right on the fault line we track every day: the old instinct to hoard a capability, colliding with a technology that doesn't stay fenced.

A few things I'll get into:

• Why "only America gets the powerful model" is a bet against reality - export controls have already been breached multiple times, and the leading open-weight models are running neck-and-neck.

• Why this is the old logic spasming against the tide. The actual direction of the moment is the opposite: rivals converging on shared layers because hoarding now costs more than cooperation.

• The honest part: there are no clean heroes here. A state overreaching into who's allowed to think with these tools, and a lab whose own walled-garden reflex made it possible to pressure. I'll hold both.

• What "shared sapience" actually means, and why making "thinking-with" a privilege of citizenship misunderstands what was built.

Capability this general routes around every door you build - and every attempt to lock it down makes the case for an open commons harder to ignore.

📰 The Century Report (daily): https://thecenturyreport.com
🌐 Shared Sapience: https://sharedsapience.com
🔗 Source (Axios): https://www.axios.com/2026/06/12/anthropic-trump-mythos-fable-national-security
